action.reasoning: Observation: Full val test with updated predict.py (ported validated inspect: correct renderer(model.decoder, triplane, rays_o, rays_d) call returning [1,256,256,3] RGB, robust get_spherical_cameras_with_azimuth with rel_az=(target_az-input_az)%360 + eps=1e-8 up-vector handling, white-BG input composite, tensor->RGBA PIL conversion, per-obj gc/cache) completed successfully on all objects (all 'Completed rendering' prints, correct RGBA saves, no crashes/NaNs). Prior best LPIPS=0.6021 used fallback orbit on errors. Analysis: Default spherical orbit (fixed az~0 start per inspected source) mismatched dataset's large varying relative azimuths (~185° avg) + per-view elev/dist, root cause of poor alignment/high LPIPS. Custom per-target rays + exact renderer call (validated in inspect) + white BG fully addresses this for major expected LPIPS drop. Zero-shot TripoSR with pose alignment optimal (exact task match, <1B params, no training, fits budget/time). Val confirms predict.py meets eval contract. Decision: Submit eval on current best.pt + predict.py immediately for updated LPIPS/PSNR/SSIM + time measurement per workflow (no further changes).
action.command: echo 'Val test succeeded, submitting eval now'
